description: A match on a sealed type ignores the exhaustiveness warning, so adding a variant later throws MatchError at runtime.
body: Failure mode: a pattern match over a sealed trait/enum omits a case (or relied on a catch-all _) and the non-exhaustiveness warning was suppressed, so when a new subtype is added the match has no branch and throws a scala.MatchError in production. Detection signals: compiler 'match may not be exhaustive' warning that is ignored; a blanket _ case on a domain ADT; a MatchError at runtime after a new variant. Do not suppress exhaustiveness warnings or hide variants behind _. Safer fallback: keep the hierarchy sealed, match every case explicitly, enable -Xfatal-warnings so a missing case fails compilation, and test each variant.
